source: broad 6-lens audit (w757bjyrv) at the 15/32 halfway mark; 60 findings, deduped vs 126 manifest, curated strongest-first. Full raw findings in task w757bjyrv.output.
note: Proves the space was NOT saturated. All entries below are capture-safe, no-WebGL, single-self-contained-file compatible. Dropped from raw set: view-transition-portfolio-nav (cross-document MPA conflicts with single-file rule). Build agents expand each brief; every brief already carries the scroll=0 signature + mobile + constraints. Pick DIVERSE batches (mix a vertical + a design-movement + a platform-primitive + a web-artifact + a generative-static).

ideas.name: SWISS GRID - International Typographic Style editorial
ideas.ref: Muller-Brockmann grid systems; Helvetica Now; Swiss poster tradition; Awwwards minimal-editorial 2026
ideas.palette: #f4f1ea paper or #ffffff + #111 ink + ONE red #e2231a; Helvetica Neue / Neue Haas only
ideas.brief: The disciplined opposite of brutalist: a rigorous 12-column Swiss grid made VISIBLE through one oversized flush-left Helvetica headline, a single hairline rule, asymmetric but mathematically-aligned blocks, and a cropped image obeying the grid. Hierarchy from scale + position + one red, never decoration. CAPTURE scroll=0: a confident flush-left display headline on a strict grid with a ruled divider and a grid-aligned image must read as deliberate Swiss precision. Mobile keeps the grid (collapse columns, keep alignment). Two-tone+red, contain media <=640px, reduced-motion honored, no em/en dashes, reuse ../assets/. Native CSS/JS only, NO GSAP.

ideas.name: DE STIJL - neoplastic grid as page architecture
ideas.ref: Mondrian; Theo van Doesburg; De Stijl 1917-1931; Bauhaus-adjacent neoplasticism
ideas.palette: #f7f4ec off-white + thick #0a0a0a black rules + primary #d4221f red / #1356a8 blue / #f2c200 yellow ONLY
ideas.brief: The viewport divided by THICK black horizontal/vertical lines into asymmetric rectangles, some filled solid primary red/blue/yellow, the rest white; content (nav, work tiles, headline) lives INSIDE the colored fields as an ideological commitment to neoplasticism. Media slots into a white cell. CAPTURE scroll=0: the asymmetric black-ruled primary-color rectangle composition must be unmistakably Mondrian/De Stijl. Mobile re-flows the rectangles vertically keeping thick rules + primaries. Reduced-motion honored, no em/en dashes, reuse ../assets/. Native CSS grid/JS only, NO GSAP.

ideas.name: CONSTRUCTIVIST - revolutionary poster as UI
ideas.ref: Rodchenko; El Lissitzky 'Beat the Whites with the Red Wedge'; Stenberg brothers film posters
ideas.palette: #ece7dd paper + #c81912 revolutionary red + #0d0d0d black; condensed heavy sans, diagonal axis
ideas.brief: Committed, heroic, geometric: a full-bleed diagonal red bar/wedge slicing the layout, oversized black condensed sans set on a 30-45deg axis, photomontage blocks, bold circular/triangular geometric markers. Not brutalist (raw/punk) - this is propaganda-poster confidence. CAPTURE scroll=0: the diagonal red wedge + giant diagonal condensed headline + geometric montage must read as Constructivist agitprop. Mobile keeps the diagonal energy (rotate-safe, no overflow). Reduced-motion honored, no em/en dashes, any inline SVG fill/stroke hex not var(), reuse ../assets/. Native CSS/JS only, NO GSAP.

ideas.name: SAUL BASS - mid-century paper-cut poster page
ideas.ref: Saul Bass title sequences + posters; Anatomy of a Murder; mid-century American modernism
ideas.palette: #efe6d2 cream + rust #c0532b + ink #1a1714 + one accent ochre #d99a2b; flat paper-cut shapes
ideas.brief: Flat geometric paper-cut shapes in 2-3 colors, a large off-center silhouette (figure, bird, abstract form), torn/hand-cut edges, a friendly mid-century sans, generous flat color fields. Distinct from seriograph (registration-offset print) - this is the cinematic Bass poster language. CAPTURE scroll=0: a big flat paper-cut silhouette in rust on cream offset left, with the title set confidently beside it, must read as a Saul Bass poster. Mobile keeps the silhouette dominant. Reduced-motion honored, no em/en dashes, inline SVG shapes hex fills, reuse ../assets/. Native CSS/SVG/JS only, NO GSAP.

ideas.name: UKIYO-E - Edo woodblock flat color + contour line

ideas.palette: #efe7d4 washi paper + Prussian blue #2a4a7f + indigo #16335c + vermilion seal #b5402e + ink contour #1c1c1c
ideas.brief: Maximally decorated within strict flat-color discipline: a hero illustration in ukiyo-e style (a Hokusai-style wave or Mt-Fuji form) built from flat Prussian-blue zones separated by bold black contour lines, a red hanko seal as accent, vertical Japanese-grid composition, washi paper grain. CAPTURE scroll=0: the flat-color contour-line woodblock hero + seal must read as Edo ukiyo-e, not generic minimal-Japanese. Mobile keeps the woodblock hero contained. Reduced-motion honored, no em/en dashes, inline SVG hex fills, reuse ../assets/ inside framed cartouches. Native CSS/SVG/JS only, NO GSAP.

ideas.name: ART DECO - gilded geometric symmetry
ideas.ref: 1920s-30s Art Deco; Cassandre posters; Chrysler Building motifs; gilded geometric luxury

ideas.brief: 1920s-30s decorative geometric luxury: a symmetrical sunburst/fan-vault SVG motif in gold on deep navy, stepped ziggurat frames, an Art-Deco display face (tall, geometric, generous tracking) centered, hairline gold rules. CAPTURE scroll=0: the centered gold sunburst motif + deco display title on navy must read as gilded Art Deco glamour. Mobile keeps symmetry (scale the motif). Reduced-motion honored, no em/en dashes, inline SVG gold hex strokes, reuse ../assets/. Native CSS/SVG/JS only, NO GSAP.

ideas.brief: 1980s Memphis maximalism: a background of black squiggly lines + confetti dots + small triangles, clashing primary+pastel geometric blocks, terrazzo texture, a chunky playful display face, asymmetric tilted cards. Joyful, anti-restraint. CAPTURE scroll=0: the squiggle+confetti pattern field with tilted colored geometric cards must read as Memphis postmodern. Mobile keeps the pattern energy (no overflow). Reduced-motion honored, no em/en dashes, inline SVG pattern hex, reuse ../assets/ in tilted frames. Native CSS/SVG/JS only, NO GSAP.

Trust engineered through layout. CAPTURE scroll=0: headline + CTA + the compliance-badge pill row + a trust score must all be visible and read as a regulated-fintech page (distinct from generic SaaS dark). Mobile stacks cleanly. Reduced-motion honored, no em/en dashes, reuse ../assets/ for logos/screens. Native CSS/JS only, NO GSAP.

CAPTURE scroll=0: the molecular hero diagram + the phase-progress pipeline table must read as a biotech investor brief, not a generic SaaS page. Mobile stacks the pipeline rows. Reduced-motion honored (bars settled), no em/en dashes, inline SVG hex (never var()), reuse ../assets/. Native CSS/SVG/JS only, NO GSAP.

CAPTURE scroll=0: headline + a partially-typed highlighted code block + install one-liner must read as a developer-tool landing. Mobile keeps the code block readable (horizontal scroll inside the block, not page overflow). Reduced-motion honored (code shown fully settled, no typing), no em/en dashes, reuse ../assets/. Native CSS/JS only, NO GSAP.

ideas.brief: The unmistakable reference-docs anatomy: a persistent left sidebar of endpoint groups with colored METHOD badges, a center column with an endpoint title + description + parameter table, and a right column with a dark code sample + a language tab strip (cURL / JS / Python) and a response block. CAPTURE scroll=0: the three-panel docs layout (nav + endpoint + code sample) must read as API reference docs, not a blog or dashboard. Mobile collapses sidebar into a top menu and stacks endpoint over code. Reduced-motion honored, no em/en dashes, reuse ../assets/ none needed (pure UI). Native CSS/JS only, NO GSAP.

ideas.name: SCHEDULE GRID - dense multi-track conference timetable

ideas.brief: A conference multi-track timetable AS the hero: horizontal day columns crossed by vertical time-slot rows (CSS grid), each cell a session card with title, speaker avatar, a track color-code, and a duration height; a sticky time rail on the left, a 'happening now' amber line, and track-filter chips. Distinct from interactive-timeline (linear narrative). CAPTURE scroll=0: the dense color-coded track grid with session cells must read as a conference schedule. Mobile switches to a single-day vertical agenda list with track dots. Reduced-motion honored, no em/en dashes, reuse ../assets/ for avatars. Native CSS grid/JS only, NO GSAP.

ideas.name: LINEUP POSTER - typographic festival bill
ideas.ref: Glastonbury/Coachella/Primavera lineup posters; letterpress music-bill typography
ideas.palette: #101012 night + acid #d8f64a + hot pink #ff3d8b + bone; OR sun-bleached #f3e9d6 + ink + red; condensed grotesk
ideas.brief: A web-native festival LINEUP poster whose typographic hierarchy IS the schedule: a giant headliner name spanning the viewport, descending tiers of smaller act names packed justified across stage columns, a date+venue badge, and bullet separators between acts. Reads like a letterpress bill. CAPTURE scroll=0: the giant headliner + packed descending act tiers must read instantly as a festival lineup poster. Mobile re-flows tiers to single column keeping the size hierarchy. Reduced-motion honored, no em/en dashes (use middots/commas), no media required. Native CSS/JS only, NO GSAP.

ideas.name: CURRICULUM - print-fidelity resume sheet
ideas.ref: Swiss CV typography; print resume conventions; A4 document-replica web pages
ideas.palette: #ffffff sheet on #ece9e2 desk + ink #161616 + one accent #2c6fad; IBM Plex Sans + Mono, tabular figures
ideas.brief: A CV/resume as a print-fidelity document: an A4-ratio white sheet centered on a desk-gray ground, a name+contact header, a left sidebar of skills as labeled bars, a right column of experience entries with date ranges aligned in a tabular rail, and a subtle paper shadow. Hover reveals a detail line per role. CAPTURE scroll=0: the centered A4 sheet with header + skills sidebar + dated experience column must read as a resume document. Mobile drops the desk and fills width single-column. Reduced-motion honored, no em/en dashes (use 'to' for ranges), optional ../assets/ headshot contained. Native CSS/JS only, NO GSAP.

ideas.name: COUNTDOWN - launch/RSVP page with live timer hero
ideas.ref: product-launch teaser pages; event RSVP microsites; segmented-display countdown convention
ideas.palette: #0a0a0f void + bone + electric accent #5b8cff or amber #e8c547; segmented display digits; Space Grotesk
ideas.brief: A pre-launch / RSVP single page: an oversized segmented-display COUNTDOWN clock (DD HH MM SS, live ticking) dominating the upper two-thirds, the event/product title beneath in tight display type, and an inline email RSVP field + CTA. One ambient motion accent. CAPTURE scroll=0: the giant countdown clock + title + RSVP field must read as a launch/countdown page. Mobile keeps the clock dominant (wrap to 2x2 segments). Reduced-motion honored (clock still shows time, no flip animation), no em/en dashes, optional ../assets/ bg. NOTE do not actually submit the form. Native CSS/JS only, NO GSAP.

ideas.name: STATUS - public service-status / uptime page
ideas.ref: Atlassian Statuspage; Stripe status; GitHub status; incident-history convention
ideas.palette: #0f1217 dark + bone + operational green #1fa97a / degraded amber #e8a93a / outage red #c0392b; Inter + Mono
ideas.brief: A public-facing service-status page (distinct from the internal ops-dashboard): a big 'All Systems Operational' banner with an overall status dot, a vertical list of services each with a status pill and a 90-day uptime bar chart (tiny daily bars colored by state), an uptime % per service, and a recent-incident history list with timestamps. CAPTURE scroll=0: the operational banner + service list with 90-day uptime bars must read as a status page. Mobile keeps the bars (shrink count). Reduced-motion honored, no em/en dashes, no media needed. Native CSS/SVG/JS only, NO GSAP.

ideas.name: REVEAL - CSS scroll-driven animation reveal page
ideas.ref: CSS scroll-driven animations (animation-timeline: view()); Chrome 2024-2025 Baseline; Codrops SDA demos
ideas.palette: #0e0e12 + bone + one accent #b6f84a or #ff5a3c; big editorial type
ideas.brief: Identity = native CSS scroll-driven animations (animation-timeline: view()) with NO scroll-position JS: every text block and image panel animates in as it crosses the viewport, fully declaratively. Distinct from parallax (1:1 scroll-position mapping) - these are viewport-CROSSING triggers. CAPTURE scroll=0: the hero is composed so the first panel sits at its settled in-view state (not mid-flight) and reads as a bold editorial statement; downpage panels reveal on cross. Mobile identical. CRITICAL prefers-reduced-motion: disable the view() animations (show settled state) since they are motion. No em/en dashes, reuse ../assets/. Native CSS/JS only, NO GSAP.

ideas.name: SPECTRUM - @property typed-gradient brand system
ideas.ref: CSS @property typed custom properties; animatable gradient hues; 2025 Baseline
ideas.palette: derived live from two @property <color> vars sweeping oklch hues over near-black; bone type
ideas.brief: Identity = @property typed custom properties making a gradient ANIMATABLE: the hero background is a gradient built from two registered @property <angle>/<color> vars, driven by a scroll or time animation so the brand gradient genuinely morphs (impossible without @property). Distinct from chromatic-arc (static conic decoration) and Y3K holo (WebGL). CAPTURE scroll=0: a rich live oklch gradient field with a confident wordmark + tagline must read as a kinetic brand identity. Mobile identical. Reduced-motion honored (freeze the gradient at a chosen stop), no em/en dashes, reuse ../assets/ optional. Native CSS/JS only, NO GSAP.

ideas.name: FILMSTRIP - zero-JS native CSS carousel
ideas.ref: CSS Carousels ::scroll-button + ::scroll-marker (Chrome 135+ 2025); scroll-snap
ideas.palette: #15110d + warm bone + copper #c9794a active marker; editorial
ideas.brief: Identity = the NEW native CSS carousel: a full-bleed horizontal scroll-snap container whose prev/next buttons are ::scroll-button() and whose dot pagination is ::scroll-marker (generated, zero JS). Distinct from ring-carousel (WebGL/JS) and marquee (auto-scroll). CAPTURE scroll=0: the first full-bleed card + the CSS-generated numbered ::scroll-marker dots + a styled ::scroll-button must read as an accessible native filmstrip. Provide a graceful non-supporting fallback (plain snap strip). Mobile identical (snap is touch-native). Reduced-motion honored, no em/en dashes, reuse ../assets/. Native CSS/JS only, NO GSAP.

ideas.name: VOXEL - CSS isometric voxel diorama hero (no WebGL)
ideas.ref: isometric CSS art; voxel dioramas; CSS 3D transforms; monument-valley-ish scenes
ideas.palette: #11131a night + warm voxel palette (coral/teal/sand tops, darker sides for shade); bone type
ideas.brief: A whole isometric VOXEL scene built purely from CSS 3D transforms (no WebGL): an island/architectural diorama of stacked cuboids with lit tops and shaded sides, a slow ambient rotate, content cards floating beside it. Distinct from the single rotating CSS-cube. CAPTURE scroll=0: a detailed isometric voxel diorama + a confident title must read as a hand-built 3D scene. Performance: keep cuboid count reasonable (target >50fps). Mobile scales the diorama to contain (no crop). Reduced-motion honored (freeze rotation), no em/en dashes, reuse ../assets/ optional on a billboard face. Native CSS/JS only, NO GSAP, NO WebGL.

ideas.brief: A full-bleed generative hero built ENTIRELY from an inline SVG feTurbulence + feColorMatrix/feDisplacement filter producing swirling contour-map-like organic terrain in one flat color + ground, with a confident wordmark over it. Static (no GPU), captures reliably. Distinct from noise-displacement-hover (cursor tilt). CAPTURE scroll=0: the organic flow-field backdrop + wordmark must read as a generative-design hero. Mobile keeps the field (re-seed via different baseFrequency). Reduced-motion honored (filter is static anyway), no em/en dashes, SVG filter primitives only, reuse ../assets/ optional. Native CSS/SVG/JS only, NO GSAP, NO WebGL.

Distinct from variable-font-pressure (scroll/cursor-driven weight). CAPTURE scroll=0: the giant specimen + visible labeled axis sliders must read as a type playground. Mobile stacks sliders under the specimen. Reduced-motion honored (no auto-animation; static specimen), no em/en dashes, use a Google variable font. Native CSS/JS only, NO GSAP.

Reduced-motion honored, no em/en dashes, reuse ../assets/ as the hero object (contain). Native CSS/JS only, NO GSAP.

Reduced-motion honored, no em/en dashes, SVG callout lines hex, reuse ../assets/. Native CSS/SVG/JS only, NO GSAP.

Distinct from riso/1-bit dither (full-image Bayer). CAPTURE scroll=0: a giant halftone-dot headline must read as printed display type, dots clearly visible at thumbnail scale (make dots coarse). ideas.name: ROSTER - competitive gaming org landing
ideas.ref: G2/T1/FaZe org sites; esports roster + sponsor-shelf conventions; condensed jersey type
ideas.palette: #0a0a0d near-black + ONE neon brand color (#39ff14 / #ff2965 / #00e5ff) + bone; condensed heavy caps
ideas.brief: An esports org landing: a full-bleed team photo/render hero with a single neon brand accent, an aggressive condensed all-caps wordmark + tagline, a horizontal ROSTER STRIP of player cards (handle, role, country flag, stat overlay) along the lower viewport, and a sponsor logo shelf. CAPTURE scroll=0: the full-bleed team hero + neon condensed headline + roster card strip must read as a competitive gaming org. Mobile keeps roster as a horizontal scroll strip. Reduced-motion honored, no em/en dashes, reuse ../assets/ for team/player imagery. Native CSS/JS only, NO GSAP.
